hook: Capital is moving — and Singapore is where it lands

slides.sub: Fed pause signals, USD/SGD pressure, and regional risk-off are redirecting capital — here's where Singapore property sits right now.

slides.lead: Three macro forces converged this week — all pointing the same direction.
slides.source: Fed statements · MAS · Bloomberg · POV Guy analysis

slides.post: SGD holding firm as USD retreats on Fed pause — strengthens Singapore's safe-haven appeal to foreign buyers.

slides.title: Three reasons capital chooses Singapore

items.body: SGD strength cuts effective entry cost for USD-denominated buyers by 1–2% in a single week.
Fed pause compresses US rate advantage — Singapore core CPI at ~2.5% looks stable by comparison.
Regional flashpoints (Taiwan Strait, South China Sea) push UHNWI diversification timelines forward.

slides.source: MAS · Fed · Bloomberg · POV Guy analysis

slides.title: SG property segments attracting flight capital

items.label: GCB / Prime Landed
items.sub: D10/D11 — low supply, hard asset store

items.label: Prime HDB / OCR
items.sub: SG-citizen driven, insulated from ABSD risk

slides.label: The macro read
slides.punch: Every time global risk spikes, Singapore's transaction data shows a lag of 60–90 days before foreign-linked caveats accelerate. We are in that lag window right now.
slides.punch_bold: The buyers who move in the lag window pay last cycle's prices.

slides.title: What the flow data is showing

items.body: SG private residential foreign-buyer caveats ticked up ~8% QoQ in Q1 2026 — early rotation signal.
GCB enquiries from Hong Kong and Indonesian HNW clients up noticeably in April per agent-network intel.
ABSD remission pipeline for qualifying entities remains open — institutional capital knows the playbook.

slides.source: URA caveats · POV Guy network · Q1 2026 data

slides.title: Let's map your
slides.sub: 30-min strategy call — we'll run the FX-adjusted numbers and show you exactly where the inbound capital is landing.

slides.title: Forward this to
slides.sub: If you know anyone sitting on cash while the world gets noisier, send them this.

caption:
When global risk spikes, Singapore real estate is usually where the money lands. 📉

• Fed pause + softer USD cuts effective entry cost for foreign buyers by 1–2% this week
• Foreign-linked caveats in SG rose ~8% QoQ in Q1 2026 — the rotation has started
• GCB and prime CCR enquiries from HK and Indonesian HNW buyers are picking up

The lag between macro fear and Singapore transaction data is 60–90 days. That window is open right now — and it closes fast.
